{{Quote|Maybe you can't prove it, but General Dodonna's son's gone bad! Any mission with him is a doom mission!|Han Solo|<ref name="doom" />}}
'''Vrad Dodonna''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and the son of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], the Alliance's top military strategist during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Although living in the shadow of his father, by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] Vrad Dodonna was considered one of the Alliance's brightest and most experienced pilots. However, Dodonna lost his faith in the Rebellion after a harrowing encounter with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Executor/Legends|Executor]]'', causing him to flee a [[Battle of Skorrupon (Galactic Civil War)|battle]] at [[Skorrupon/Legends|Skorrupon]] in terror. Dodonna later planned to trade a valuable [[power gem]] to the Imperials to bargain for his own life, but instead sacrificed his life in a [[Death/Legends|suicide]] run in the ''Executor'', in doing so turning aside an Imperial attack on the Rebels' [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] [[Great Temple/Legends|base]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Luke! Tell my father... whatever else I've done... I've tried to make up for it.|Vrad Dodonna before his sacrifice|<ref name="doom" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Vrad Dodonna was the son of [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], a former [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Republic Navy/Legends|naval]] officer who came out of retirement to become the preeminent military tactician for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="mts">''[[The Movie Trilogy Sourcebook]]''</ref> Dodonna was raised by his father at his estate on [[Brelor]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]], which had been granted to the Dodonna family as a gift from the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] upon his retirement. The elder Dodonna did not support the Imperial regime, but also initially resisted overtures to join the Rebel Alliance—however, after he and his son narrowly escaped an attempt on their lives from [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order|COMPNOR]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]], they entered the war on the Rebel side.<ref name="negtc" />

[[File:ExecutorVrad-CSW.png|thumb|left|300px|Vrad Dodonna engages the ''Executor'' at Yavin.]]
While Jan took his place as one of the Alliance's top military leaders, Vrad became known as one of the fighting force's most promising young [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]].<ref name="negtc" /> By shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Dodonna had more combat experience than almost any pilot in the Rebellion, and was at [[Skorrupon]] when the Rebel Alliance had one of its first encounters with the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Executor/Legends|Executor]]'', the massive [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Overwhelmed with fear at the sight of the vessel, Dodonna fled the engagement, leaving the rest of the Rebel forces behind to be annihilated. Although Dodonna was briefly feared [[Death/Legends|dead]], he later returned to the Alliance's [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] in a damaged [[Vrad Dodonna's scout ship|scout ship]], claiming that he had been attacked by the enemy and needed to make emergency repairs to survive.<ref name="doom" />

The only survivor of the Rebel [[Battle of Skorrupon (Galactic Civil War)|defeat]] at Skorrupon, Dodonna was feted as a hero upon his return to Yavin. However, his story earned some suspicion: [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], hero of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], had entered the [[Skorrupon system/Legends|Skorrupon system]] shortly after the battle and had seen Dodonna's undamaged ship flee. Nevertheless, Rebel leadership was preoccupied with forming a plan to take out the ''Executor'', which was en route to attack Yavin—over Skywalker's objections, Dodonna volunteered to fly an [[Power-gem attack ship|attack ship]] loaded with a [[power gem]] into the ''Executor'', an extremely dangerous plan that his father hoped could cause catastrophic damage to the cruiser. Skywalker was named Dodonna's backup pilot, and slotted to fly the mission alongside him. However, angered by Skywalker questioning his story of escaping the ''Executor'', Dodonna picked a fight with Skywalker that ended with Skywalker gaining the upper hand and Dodonna admitting that he had fled the battle.<ref name="doom" />

Ultimately, Dodonna had no intent to actually face the Empire. Dodonna instead planned to ditch Skywalker on an uninhabited [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] and instead parley with the Empire, believing that the Alliance had no chance to win the war—Dodonna hoped that the power gem would allow him to bargain with his own life. However, Vader sensed Skywalker's presence through the Force and reversed course, abandoning his attack vector on Yavin to target the man who had destroyed the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. Skywalker pled with Dodonna to resume the mission, and ultimately got through to him—Dodonna went back up in the attack ship alone and did a suicide run on the ''Executor'', doing grievous damage to the destroyer and forcing it to flee the Yavin system. In turn, Skywalker concealed Dodonna's treachery upon his safe return to Yavin, allowing the dead pilot to be remembered for a heroic sacrifice.<ref name="doom" /> However, his son's death caused his father Jan Dodonna to fall into a deep depression, ultimately resulting in his semi-retirement.<ref name="mts" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|You can afford to be self-righteous, Skywalker…you've got the Force with you. All I ever had was being General Dodonna's son!|Vrad Dodonna to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="doom" />}}
Although his father was renowned as one of the Rebellion's finest leaders, Vrad Dodonna could be hostile, hotheaded and even treacherous. Dodonna was nevertheless a skilled pilot, with an extremely high level of experience in ship-against-ship combat, but the encounter with the ''Executor'' was unlike anything Dodonna had experienced before—Dodonna later said that seeing a ship that massive and overwhelming had broken something inside him, and convinced him that the Rebel Alliance had no chance to win the war. Believing an ex-Rebel would not be allowed to survive after the Alliance's pending defeat, Dodonna initially elected to save his own skin and parley with the Empire. However, Luke Skywalker managed to inspire Dodonna to finish his final mission, and he sacrificed himself in an effort to, in his own mind, make up for the mistakes he had made in the past.<ref name="doom" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vrad Dodonna appeared in ''[[Doom Mission]]'', an arc of the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' comic strip]] written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Al Williamson]], and released in [[1983]].<ref name="doom" /> Dodonna was also mentioned in [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 21|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 21]], where he was erroneously stated to be Jan Dodonna's nephew.<ref name="ff">{{FactFile|21|SKY 9-12|Luke Skywalker}}</ref>
